topics.title: Neural Markers of Chronic Pain Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on pain disorders such as fibromyalgia, chronic migraine, and trigeminal neuralgia, emphasizing pain perception, chronic pain mechanisms, and central sensitization. It also includes brain-based markers and cerebral perfusion findings linked to these conditions.

keywords: symptom dimensions show distinct nonlinear regional volume response
topics.title: ADHD Subtypes and Developmental Brain Differences
topics.description: This cluster focuses on ADHD heterogeneity, especially symptom dimensions, subtypes, and comorbid features across development. It links these clinical patterns to structural, functional, and electrophysiological brain differences in children and youth.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism brain development and clinical variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism across childhood and youth, emphasizing brain abnormalities, developmental trajectories, and links between neural organization and behavior. It also highlights clinical heterogeneity, including autistic traits, subgroups, and early risk markers.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
topics.title: Neural and Reward Mechanisms in Music
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music listening, training, and participation shape reward, emotion, memory, and neural connectivity. It also includes studies of brain pathways and mechanisms supporting musical skill retention and engagement.

topics.title: Noninvasive Brain Stimulation and Network Effects
topics.description: This cluster focuses on transcranial stimulation approaches and their effects on cortical excitability, connectivity, and brain rhythms across clinical and cognitive contexts. It includes studies in stroke and MCI, often using EEG or fMRI to assess mechanisms and efficacy.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on ketamine administration and its dose-dependent effects on perception, therapeutic response, and brain network function. The abstracts emphasize alterations in functional connectivity, neural information dynamics, and connectome-level modeling across healthy adults and major depression.

topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin and related psychedelics alter functional connectivity, cortical dynamics, and large-scale brain network organization. It also includes EEG- and network-based analyses used to characterize psychedelic-induced changes in brain activity.

topics.title: Neural Correlates of Tinnitus and Suppression
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural basis of tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes, tinnitus loudness, and suppression. It also highlights structural and neurochemical findings across auditory cortex, prefrontal cortex, and related brain measures.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
development and evaluation of an intervention for adolescents
topics.title: Neural and Intervention Studies in Dyscalculia
topics.description: This cluster focuses on developmental dyscalculia and related math-learning difficulties, especially how training and intervention affect arithmetic performance and brain organization. The abstracts emphasize numerical networks, parietal activation, and neural adaptation during math learning.

keywords: long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
topics.title: Exercise Effects on Brain Connectivity and Structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on how exercise and physical activity relate to brain-body interactions, functional connectivity, and white matter changes across adulthood. It also includes evidence from aerobic and social dance interventions in healthy older and sedentary younger adults.

topics.title: Adolescent Substance Exposure and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent brain development and functional network connectivity relate to substance exposure, craving, dependence, and later use trajectories. It also includes familial and social risk factors, prenatal exposure, and predictions of teenage nicotine use across substance-related disorders.

topics.title: Neural Predictors of Alcohol Use Risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain imaging measures relate to alcohol use, misuse, and early initiation, especially in adolescence and young adults. It highlights functional and white matter markers, connectivity changes, and predictive neural features linked to alcohol-related risk.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Body Odor Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how smell and body odor are processed in the brain, including olfactory identification, brain responses, and related neural mechanisms. It also includes clinical and behavioral aspects such as olfactory dysfunction, congenital anosmia, fear, and self body odor comparison.

keywords: homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate
topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Bases of Food Choice
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain systems, appetite-related signals, and cognitive control shape food choice and eating behavior across conditions such as obesity, anorexia nervosa, and binge eating disorder. It also includes work on attentional focus, distracted eating, and homeostatic-hedonic mechanisms linked to food decisions.

topics.title: Language Processing in Multilingual Reading Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on language processing and control in multilingual and bilingual speakers, with emphasis on reading, naming, and linguistic discrimination. It also includes dyslexia-related work and neural accounts such as neural dynamics, noise, and fMRI-based language findings.

keywords: neural encoding of distinct speaker traits a hierarchical framework
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Speech and Auditory Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports speech perception, voice identity, auditory memory, and predictive processing in natural and challenging listening conditions. It also includes work on cortical organization, neural dynamics, and individual differences measured with fMRI, MEG, and intracranial electrophysiology.

topics.title: MRI Biomarkers in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy
topics.description: Studies in this cluster focus on structural and diffusion MRI markers of temporal lobe epilepsy, especially mesial temporal sclerosis, hippocampal abnormalities, cortical atrophy, and white matter changes. They also address multimodal differentiation and network reorganization for improving diagnosis and characterization of refractory disease.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language functions are lateralized and reorganized after injury or developmental disruption, especially in aphasia and stroke. It also emphasizes structural disconnection, lesion-symptom evidence, and individual variability in language dominance.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
topics.title: Dynamic Networks in Epilepsy Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy-related brain network alterations, especially dynamic functional connectivity, thalamic connectivity, and epileptogenic zone localization in focal and temporal lobe epilepsy. It also highlights how these network measures relate to surgical outcome and individualized biomarkers.

topics.title: Epilepsy Localization and Presurgical Network Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones in epilepsy, especially in presurgical evaluation and pediatric or nonlesional cases. It also emphasizes connectome- and EEG-based network analyses for characterizing epileptic activity and improving surgical outcome prediction.

keywords: prediction of brain responses reveal emotional effects in sentence processing
topics.title: Naturalistic Language Processing and Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports sentence, narrative, and proverb comprehension in naturalistic language settings, with attention to prediction, uncertainty, and emotional effects. It also includes work on language-network organization and the neural architecture underlying abstraction deficits and interpretation.

topics.title: Neural Architecture of Language Comprehension
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports language and speech comprehension across reading, listening, and narrative contexts. It also highlights lateralization, predictive processing, semantics, and learning-related differences across subjects and languages.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound for Essential Tremor Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor and related motor improvement outcomes. It emphasizes cerebellothalamic network mapping, connectivity fingerprints, and longitudinal functional and structural changes linked to durable treatment effects.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation, especially subthalamic nucleus stimulation, in Parkinson disease and related patient cohorts. The abstracts emphasize response prediction, outcome stratification, and the role of preoperative imaging signatures in personalizing therapy.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma-Related Brain Network Reorganization
topics.description: These studies focus on how glioma and brain tumor surgery alter brain connectivity, network organization, and structural disconnection. They also examine how preoperative network features and reorganization patterns relate to postoperative deficits, functional recovery, and cognitive impairment.

topics.title: Human Sleep Dynamics and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on human sleep, including age-related changes, hypersomnolence, insomnia, and central sleep disorders. It emphasizes dynamic brain activity and connectivity patterns during sleep, often studied with fMRI, EEG, and accelerometry.

topics.title: Neural Correlates of Hallucinations in Parkinsonian Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on hallucinations in Parkinson disease and Lewy body disease, emphasizing connectivity changes, brain metabolic alterations, and atrophy associated with symptom emergence. It also includes biomarker-oriented studies of visual and auditory hallucinations, including minor hallucinations and awareness of visual hallucinations.

keywords: distinct effects of stroke lesion topography on multiple sensorimotor tracts
early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
advanced diffusion mri captures therapy relate brain plasticity
topics.title: Stroke Recovery and Motor Network Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke and related motor disorders reshape motor networks, sensorimotor tracts, and functional connectivity during recovery. It also emphasizes prediction of post-stroke motor outcomes and the role of structural and network-level plasticity in rehabilitation.

keywords: network level structural disconnection mapping of post stroke functional deficits
topics.title: Stroke Recovery and Network Disconnection
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how stroke-related lesions and structural disconnections shape cognitive, language, and functional outcomes across acute and chronic stages. It emphasizes prediction, recovery, and post-stroke neuroplasticity using lesion- and network-based analyses.

topics.title: Brain Network Reorganization After Injury
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how traumatic and chronic brain injury alter structural and functional brain networks, including cortical, limbic, and default mode systems. Studies use multimodal neuroimaging and network modeling to characterize reorganization and connectivity changes linked to recovery and outcome.

keywords: traumatic brain injury in adolescent brain cognitive development study
topics.title: Traumatic Brain Injury and Brain Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on traumatic brain injury and related impacts from concussion, repetitive head impacts, and blast exposure across youth and adolescent populations. It emphasizes white matter microstructure, network alterations, and MRI-based findings in both clinical and sports-related contexts.

topics.title: Attention and engagement measurement methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on techniques for assessing attentional control, task engagement, and cognitive engagement using eye tracking, pupillometric biomarkers, and load dependent eeg dynamics. It also includes neurofeedback and open source toolbox development for analysis and modeling, including deep neural networks.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Brain Network Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on mindfulness and related meditation practices and how they shape brain network organization, coupling, and connectivity. The abstracts emphasize functional and structural brain changes, including network efficiency, hub properties, and white matter plasticity.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: OCD, Connectivity, and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder across youth and adolescent samples, with emphasis on compulsivity, comorbid anxiety, and CBT outcomes. It also highlights frontostriatal and striatal connectivity, brain activation, and neural predictors of treatment response from large neuroimaging studies.

keywords: brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Glaucoma Neurodegeneration and Brain Correlates
topics.description: This cluster focuses on glaucoma-related neurodegeneration and how retinal, optic nerve, and visual cortex measures relate to broader brain structural changes. It also includes normative and data-driven approaches for identifying glaucoma phenotypes and mapping disease patterns across visual pathways.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Neural Bases of Reward and Risk Decisions
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural and computational mechanisms underlying decision making, reward processing, and risk-related behavior. It also highlights curiosity, exploration-exploitation tradeoffs, and prediction-error signaling in shaping learning and choices.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal Effects on Midlife Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how menstrual cycling, menopause, pregnancy, and hormone therapy relate to brain structure, connectivity, and neural activity in females. It highlights midlife and perimenopausal changes, including hippocampal and gray matter effects, as well as symptom-related conditions such as dysmenorrhea.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Cognitive Fatigue
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue across conditions such as multiple sclerosis and long covid, with emphasis on brain-network alterations and clinical symptoms. The abstracts focus on functional connectivity, multimodal assessment, and biomarkers for localizing fatigue-related neural changes.

topics.title: Laminar Myelin and Iron Imaging
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine cortical lamination, myelin, and iron-related microstructural changes using quantitative MRI and related contrasts. The abstracts focus on linking laminar correspondence and accumulation patterns to noninvasive biomarkers of brain microstructure.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
individualized mapping of functional brain networks
predictive modeling for reliable cognitive outcomes
topics.title: Aging and Functional Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain organization, connectivity, and dynamics change across the lifespan and in healthy aging. It also includes individualized and predictive mapping approaches aimed at explaining cognitive variability and reliable outcomes in older adulthood.

keywords: local brain aging in cognitively normal
topics.title: Cognitive Aging and Brain Structure
topics.description: This cluster focuses on age-related brain changes in cognitively normal adults, including cortical thickness, white matter, and brain age estimation. It also emphasizes longitudinal and normative approaches to understanding brain health across adulthood and late adulthood.

topics.title: Brain and Glycemic Effects in Type 2 Diabetes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how type 2 diabetes and glycemic dysregulation relate to brain structure, function, and cognitive outcomes. It includes youth-onset disease, acute hyperglycemia, and imaging findings such as white matter and morphometric changes.

keywords: network connectivity patterns associate with metabolic syndrome and
topics.title: Brain Aging, Health, and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain ageing and its links to body health, metabolic syndrome, and cognitive vulnerability across the lifespan. It also includes network connectivity and age-estimation approaches used to study structural and multimodal brain changes.

topics.title: Aging, Vascular Health, and Brain Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and vascular or cardiometabolic risk relate to brain structure, white matter disease, and cognitive decline. It also includes mechanisms such as neuroinflammation, cerebral hemodynamics, and neurodegeneration associated with dementia risk.

keywords: the brain network underlying social participation a multimodal
the reinforcement sensitivity theory latent mri systems
topics.title: Brain Networks of Social Participation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on multimodal and connectome-based investigations of how intrinsic brain network organization relates to social participation, loneliness, social navigation, and emotion. It also includes reinforcement sensitivity theory and measurement-invariance work linking latent MRI systems to individual differences in young adults.

keywords: adaptive interplay of action observation and mentalizing networks during ambiguous social perception
developmental trajectories of fairness perception an eeg study
daily experiences sculpt neural representations of other an intensive longitudinal fmri study
active gaze decodes dynamic neural states
topics.title: Naturalistic Social Cognition and Mentalizing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how social cognition, empathy, and mentalizing systems support perception and behavior in ambiguous, naturalistic, and developmental contexts. It also includes studies of fairness, action observation, and longitudinal neural changes across adolescence and daily experience.

topics.title: Neural Basis of Visual Mental Imagery
topics.description: This cluster centers on visual mental imagery and its subjective variability, including aphantasia, vividness, and individual differences. It also links imagery to neural correlates in the default mode network, early visual cortex, and episodic or future simulation.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
multi site multi modal mrs study
topics.title: Brain Metabolic Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping human brain metabolism with MRSI and connectomics to characterize cortical metabolic connectivity, gradient organization, and metabolite-specific network signatures. It also links neurometabolic patterns to integration, development, and disease.

topics.title: Neurometabolic Imaging of Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging approaches for quantifying brain metabolism and its coupling to functional activity across states such as sleep, consciousness, and stress. It includes studies of default mode network activity, glucose metabolism, and altered metabolic patterns in neurotrauma and other disorders.

topics.title: Anxiety and Amygdala Connectivity Under Stress
topics.description: This cluster focuses on anxiety-related disorders and stress responses, emphasizing amygdala structure and connectivity, prefrontal-amygdala pathways, and functional coupling. It also includes brain signal variability and fMRI measures linked to individual differences in emotional distress and resilience.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Multiple Sclerosis Lesion and Structural Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiple sclerosis, especially lesion evolution, disease severity, disability, and cognitive outcomes. It also includes structural MRI and diffusion-based approaches for detecting white matter abnormalities and supporting diagnosis and prognosis.

topics.title: Fear, Anxiety, and Amygdala Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and physiological mechanisms of fear and anxiety, especially conditioning, extinction, and threat response in the amygdala and autonomic nervous system. It also includes related aversive learning and clinically relevant phenomena such as misophonia.

topics.title: Structural Brain Changes in REM Sleep Behavior Disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and microstructural brain abnormalities associated with REM sleep behavior disorder and isolated REM sleep behavior disorder, including links to phenoconversion risk. Studies emphasize degeneration, atrophy, gray matter, white matter microstructural changes, and brainstem abnormalities.

topics.title: Brain Development and Environmental Exposure
topics.description: This cluster centers on how environmental exposures and urban context relate to brain health, structure, and development across childhood, adolescence, and aging. It includes longitudinal and connectivity-based studies of exposure effects, cognitive performance, and hippocampal outcomes.

keywords: optimize retinotopic mapping for visual field coverage
topics.title: Ultra-High-Field High-Resolution Visual fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on ultra-high-field fMRI approaches for mapping visual cortex with very high spatial resolution and improved BOLD sensitivity. It emphasizes retinotopic and population receptive field mapping, visual field coverage, and coil/sensitivity optimization at 7T and higher.

keywords: contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
topics.title: Sex Differences in Adolescent Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how puberty and adolescent development relate to sex differences in functional and structural brain organization, especially during cognitive maturation. It highlights network-based analyses of connectivity, cortical organization, and white matter reorganization across youth.

topics.title: Sex Differences Across Brain Development and Diversity
topics.description: This cluster centers on sex-related variation in brain structure and developmental trajectories across the lifespan, including links to prenatal androgen exposure and morphometric measures. It also includes human diversity and social-identity subgroups, with some cross-species comparisons of sex differences.

keywords: motor learning through imitation of fully visible
representational organization of hand actions across motor execution
functional upper limb motor representation relation
cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
topics.title: Action Observation and Grasp Motor Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how observed and executed hand actions are represented in motor systems, especially for grasping, imitation, and visuomotor control. It also includes work on neural organization and transformation of motor representations using fMRI, MVPA, and oscillatory measures.

keywords: early neural response to emotional speech
topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Emotion Processing
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how the brain represents and processes emotions across faces, speech, and naturalistic stimuli, with emphasis on valence, arousal, and dynamic affective changes. It also includes studies of early neural responses, attentional effects, and representational geometry of emotion.

keywords: cortical depth dependent affective touch processing
cortex to cervical spinal cord immersive vr
causal contribution of primary motor cortex
topics.title: Sensorimotor Bases of Body Ownership and Touch
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and perceptual mechanisms underlying body ownership, kinesthesia, and crossmodal tactile processing, often in the context of illusion paradigms. It also includes work probing sensorimotor cortex contributions, affective touch, and links between subjective experience and physiological state.

keywords: effect of epidural spinal stimulation on sensorimotor rhythms
topics.title: Sensorimotor maps and proprioceptive stimulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how somatosensory and proprioceptive stimulation, movement, and spinal interventions shape sensorimotor representations in cortex and related networks. It also includes work on topographic organization, body maps, and sensorimotor rhythms in relation to learning and stimulation.

topics.title: Maternal Exposures and Infant Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how maternal exposures, including gestational diabetes and prenatal substance exposure, relate to fetal, neonatal, and preterm brain connectivity and later cognitive outcomes. It also includes studies of altered claustrum and thalamo-cortical/subcortical connectivity and developmental connectome maturation.

keywords: dynamic developmental trajectories of emotion neural modules
topics.title: Neural Signatures of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation is represented in brain activity, including neural modules, spatiotemporal signatures, and links to interoception. The abstracts also emphasize developmental change, fMRI and EEG evidence, and functional patterns involving amygdala and ventral striatum activity.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Functional Connectivity and Psychopathology
topics.description: This cluster examines how functional connectivity and network measures relate to psychopathology across psychiatric disorders and clinical phenotypes, often using transdiagnostic frameworks. It also emphasizes the stability and reproducibility of brain-behavior and brain-psychopathology associations in multisite resting-state fMRI studies.

topics.title: Inflammation and Brain Signatures in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder in relation to peripheral inflammation, immune-metabolic links, and biomarker signatures. It also includes brain connectivity findings such as amygdala-frontal and default mode network changes.

keywords: multimodal indicators of mood congruent emotional processing bias
estimation of uncertainty of socio affective stimuli
topics.title: Transdiagnostic Affective Processing and Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on altered emotion regulation and affective processing across mood and psychotic disorders, with emphasis on brain activation and connectivity changes. It also includes transdiagnostic analyses of uncertainty, socio-affective stimuli, and mood-congruent processing bias.

keywords: neural correlates of state and trait emotion regulation a large scale fmri study
topics.title: Neural Basis of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates of emotion regulation, especially cognitive reappraisal, state and trait differences, and amygdala regulation. It also highlights large-scale fMRI efforts, social support, neurodevelopment, and the search for a reliable neural signature of regulation ability.

topics.title: Early Brain Network Development in Neonates
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative and atypical early brain development, especially in neonates and preterm infants, using multimodal neuroimaging to study evolving functional organization. It highlights developmental changes in network gradients, dynamic connectivity, and individualized parcellation across the perinatal period.

topics.title: Developmental Functional Connectivity in Youth
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain functional networks and connectomes change across childhood and adolescence, with emphasis on individual variability and network reorganization. It also includes naturalistic and resting-state fMRI approaches for characterizing developmental trajectories in youth.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
multi scale dynamics of cognitive network engagement
topics.title: Aging, Cognitive Control, and EEG Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and cognitive demands shape executive control, working memory, and inhibitory failure across tasks. It also emphasizes EEG markers and oscillatory dynamics, including alpha activity, aperiodic signals, and multiscale brain-state changes.

keywords: infant laminar cortical myelination associated with behavior and environment
topics.title: White Matter and Myelin Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how white matter and cortical microstructure mature across infancy and early childhood, including laminar and tract-specific developmental trajectories. It also highlights links between myelin development, behavior, and environmental influences in typical development.

topics.title: Genetic and Brain Connectivity in Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on shared genetic influences and brain-structure/connectivity patterns across schizophrenia and related psychiatric disorders. It emphasizes genomic associations, polygenic risk, and structural-functional brain variation linked to psychopathology.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging signatures of data driven symptom phenotypes
developmental brain network trajectories differentiate resilience
topics.title: Neuroimaging Subtypes and Prediction in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and psychosis spectrum research using multimodal neuroimaging to identify symptom phenotypes, brain subtypes, and prediction models. It also includes work on treatment response, resilience, and developmental brain network trajectories.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Spectrum Circuits and Symptoms
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychosis-spectrum conditions, including early and high-risk states, subtypes, and 22q11.2 deletion syndrome. It emphasizes cognitive and hallucination-related symptoms alongside altered brain connectivity and circuit-level abnormalities.

keywords: subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
right putaman plasticity specifically underpin psychomotor improvement
topics.title: Connectivity and symptom change in depression therapy
topics.description: This cluster centers on how electroconvulsive and seizure-based therapies alter functional and effective connectivity in depression, especially treatment-resistant and melancholic cases. It highlights links between subcallosal cingulate circuitry, putaminal plasticity, and clinical outcomes such as remission and psychomotor improvement.

keywords: brain network controllability predicts response to connectivity guide rtm
topics.title: Personalized Neuromodulation for Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression, especially treatment resistant depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on predicting and improving treatment response. It highlights personalized and connectivity-guided neuromodulation approaches, including target selection and network controllability.

topics.title: Resting-State Connectivity and Sensitivity Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on resting-state fMRI and intrinsic connectivity analyses in rodent and mouse models, especially motor and subcortical networks. A major emphasis is improving sensitivity, reliability, and validation through methods such as BrainSync, coactivation pattern analysis, and awake imaging.

keywords: genetic basis of human hippocampal organization genome wide analysis of multimodal imaging traits
topics.title: Genetics of Brain Structure Across Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on the genetic architecture of human brain anatomy, especially hippocampal organization, cortical structure, and related multimodal imaging traits. It also includes developmental and population-specific variation, with links to CNV burden and early markers of neuropsychiatric disorder.

keywords: the first six years of life
topics.title: Brain Structural Development Across Early Life
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain structure changes from infancy through adolescence, including cortical thickness and subcortical gray development. It also emphasizes variation linked to birthweight, gestational age, preterm birth, and heterogeneous neurodevelopmental pathways.

topics.title: Human Visual Cortex Retinotopic Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on the organization of the human visual cortex, especially retinotopic mapping, V1/V2 structure, and dorsal stream specialization. It also includes work on spatial layout, population receptive field properties, and large-scale connectomic relationships in visual areas.

keywords: resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
brainstem cortex ans effective connectivity contributes
topics.title: Brain-Heart Coupling in Resting Neurophysiology
topics.description: This cluster centers on interactions between cardiac/autonomic signals and brain dynamics, especially in resting-state fMRI and heartbeat-evoked responses. It also includes cerebrospinal fluid oscillations, interoception, and network-level physiological fluctuations across health and disease.

keywords: spatial neural modeling of hemodynamic response functions
topics.title: Modeling Hemodynamic Signals in fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for estimating and modeling hemodynamic response functions and related BOLD signal components in fMRI data. It includes joint learning, spatial neural modeling, and multi-echo or anatomical assessments across human spinal cord and awake mouse studies.

keywords: the brain age approach to identify psychological resilience
topics.title: Neurodevelopmental resilience and brain-behavior links
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental neuroscience studies of resilience, psychopathology, and brain-behavior associations across childhood and adolescence. It highlights longitudinal, family, and social-context influences, including neural synchrony and brain-age based approaches.

topics.title: MRI Segmentation Reliability and Harmonization
topics.description: This cluster centers on evaluating MRI brain segmentation pipelines for hippocampal and subcortical measures, with emphasis on longitudinal estimation and test-retest reliability. It also highlights harmonization and robustness across datasets, scanners, and sites.

keywords: traumatic brain injury and post traumatic
topics.title: PTSD Connectivity and Trauma-Related Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on PTSD and related trauma exposures, including combat, childhood trauma, intimate partner violence, and traumatic brain injury. It also emphasizes connectivity-based brain signatures and prediction of PTSD-related outcomes.

topics.title: Neurobiological resilience to early adversity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early life stress and childhood trauma relate to resilience, vulnerability, and later mental illness. It emphasizes neurobiological signatures, brain structure and connectivity, and protective influences such as memory suppression and polygenic factors.

keywords: progressive disruption of directed brain connectivity across
shared age specific structural covariance network alterations
topics.title: Neuroimaging markers of adolescent depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurobiological alterations associated with adolescent depression, suicidal ideation, and suicidal behavior. It emphasizes brain connectivity, structural covariance, hippocampal and white matter measures, and related network dysfunction across development.

topics.title: Brain Age Biomarkers for Alzheimer Detection
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain age estimation and brain aging as biomarkers for identifying Alzheimer-related disease and cognitive decline. It also includes work on regional prediction, diagnostic utility, and subtype differentiation using MRI-based models.

keywords: meta learning improve cross site depression severity prediction
topics.title: EEG Biomarkers for Depression Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on electroencephalography-based biomarkers and machine learning methods for diagnosing and predicting depression severity, with emphasis on cross-site generalization. It also highlights harmonization, feature selection, and connectivity analyses to improve reproducibility and individual prediction.

topics.title: Depression heterogeneity and brain connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related mood disorders, emphasizing heterogeneity in clinical subtypes, symptom profiles, and treatment response. It links depressive phenotypes with brain connectivity, dynamic network changes, and functional abnormalities.

topics.title: White Matter Markers in Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter abnormalities, hyperintensities, and integrity changes across neurodegenerative disorders and age-related neuropathology. It also includes biomarker-oriented multicenter studies spanning Alzheimer spectrum, FTLD, ALS, Parkinson disease, and small vessel disease.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau in Alzheimer Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how amyloid and tau relate to progression across the Alzheimer continuum, including heterogeneity, resilience, and biological-clinical mismatch. It emphasizes biomarker progression and frameworks linking amyloid tau neurodegeneration to disease staging.

keywords: hierarchical graph base tractography across large scale synchrotron brain images
topics.title: Subcortical Connectivity Mapping and Tractography
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping subcortical structures and white matter pathways using tractography, graph-based approaches, and automated segmentation. It also emphasizes large-scale brain image analysis and the identification of key nuclei and connectivity hubs.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Anesthesia, Consciousness, and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how anesthetics alter consciousness and anesthesia depth through changes in EEG complexity, entropy, and functional connectivity. It also highlights thalamocortical and network-level mechanisms underlying propofol and sevoflurane effects.

topics.title: Prefrontal Networks and Cognitive Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on how prefrontal and cingulate circuits support cognitive control, attention, and working memory. It emphasizes structural and functional connectivity, hemispheric specialization, and individualized network organization in the human brain.

topics.title: Neuroimaging-Based Psychiatric and Clinical Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging and machine learning approaches for classifying psychiatric and neurological conditions and predicting clinical outcomes. It includes applications such as schizophrenia and neurodegenerative disease classification, brain age prediction, and outcome prediction from MRI and fMRI data.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Visualization Platforms
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, browser-based tools and platforms for neuroimaging visualization, data handling, and workflow support. It emphasizes interactive multi-modal 3D viewing, portable deployment, and clinically actionable pipelines.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: Neural Representations in Human Vision
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human fMRI and deep neural network analyses are used to study neural representations of visual objects, faces, and scenes. It also links representational alignment and hierarchy to behavioral performance and dynamic processing in the human visual system.

topics.title: Cross-Species Brain Mapping Toolkits
topics.description: This cluster centers on tools and frameworks for multiscale, multimodal brain mapping across species, including mouse and human neuroimaging spaces. It emphasizes atlas construction, data processing, visualization, and structural/neurostructural analyses.

topics.title: Brain Atlas Construction and Cytoarchitectonic Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on constructing and refining brain atlases, with emphasis on cytoarchitectonic mapping, parcellation, and registration across species and coordinate frameworks. It includes work on human, primate, and cerebellar atlas resources as well as segmentation and reconstruction from histological and imaging data.

topics.title: Neurochemical Organization of Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how neurochemical systems are organized across the human and macaque brain and how they relate to cortical structure and large-scale network integration. It also includes studies of receptor density, inhibitory signaling, and individual differences in neurotransmission.

keywords: high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
progressive multi stage framework for longitudinal infant brain network completion
deep learning base neonatal brain age prediction
topics.title: Deep Learning for Infant Brain MRI Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning methods for infant and neonatal brain MRI, including segmentation, brain age prediction, and longitudinal network completion. It also covers robust processing frameworks and clinical applications for developmental assessment.

topics.title: Developmental Brain Folding and Surface Atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development, especially cortical folding, gyrification, and fetal-to-neonatal developmental trajectories. It also includes atlas construction and surface reconstruction resources for studying cortical and cerebellar anatomy across development.

topics.title: Fetal Brain MRI Analysis and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on fetal brain MRI analysis workflows and reproducible preprocessing approaches for fetal imaging data. It also addresses fetal brain development and microstructural organization across gestation using diffusion, fMRI, and multiparametric MRI.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Subtypes and Nigral Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related neurodegenerative subtypes, with emphasis on substantia nigra changes, iron accumulation, and alpha synuclein pathology. It also highlights multimodal MRI biomarkers and progression modeling for characterizing disease heterogeneity over time.

topics.title: Parkinson disease phenotypes and brain network dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and Lewy body disease heterogeneity, especially motor and neuropsychiatric phenotypes. It emphasizes brain network dynamics, dynamic functional connectivity, and biomarker-oriented neuroimaging approaches for subtype and clinical characterization.

topics.title: AI Methods for Neuroimaging and Biomarker Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and explainable AI approaches for neuroimaging, including PET/MRI synthesis, preprocessing, and interpretation. It also covers biomarker-based prediction of neuropathology and degeneration in neurodegenerative disorders.

keywords: foundation models identify eeg biomarkers for alzheimer 's
topics.title: AI Methods for Early Dementia Detection
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning and deep learning approaches for detecting Alzheimer disease and related dementias from imaging, EEG, and multimodal clinical data. It also emphasizes explainability, federated learning, and uncertainty quantification for more reliable diagnosis and prognosis.

keywords: distributional and centile calibration of dti normative models as training sample sizes increase
topics.title: Neuroimaging harmonization and normative model calibration
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for harmonizing multisite neuroimaging data and evaluating their effectiveness across batch and site effects. It also emphasizes calibration, validation, and generalization of normative models in real-world and multi-site settings.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging and Physiological Data Tools
topics.description: This cluster centers on open platforms, data warehouses, and analysis toolboxes for neuroimaging, EEG, and physiological data. It also emphasizes annotation, quality assessment, and transdiagnostic psychiatric research applications.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging infrastructure, data standardization, and collaborative platforms that support reproducible analysis across sites and consortia. It emphasizes tools and frameworks for BIDS conversion, containerization, shared execution environments, and cross-border data sharing.

topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity Biomarkers in Alzheimer’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease, mild cognitive impairment, and related cognitive decline across aging and disease stages. The abstracts emphasize dynamic brain connectivity, default mode network changes, and biomarker development for diagnosis and patient-specific characterization.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
topics.title: Preclinical Alzheimer Network and Neurochemical Changes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on functional and structural brain alterations in preclinical Alzheimer disease, including dysconnectivity, network stability, and medial temporal lobe pathway disruption. It also highlights neurochemical and pathological drivers such as tau and dopamine, with an emphasis on imaging-based markers.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity and cerebral perfusion under baseline, resting-state, and provoked conditions such as hypercapnia, oxygen breathing, and caffeine. The phrases emphasize imaging-based assessment of regional neurovascular dynamics and perfusion physiology, often using ASL and BOLD-derived methods.

topics.title: High-Field Cerebral Perfusion and Vascular Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-resolution MRI approaches for mapping cerebral blood flow, blood volume, and vascular structure, especially at 7T and in layer-specific or zero-TE fMRI contexts. It also includes software and post-processing tools for visualizing and analyzing perfusion and vascular data.

keywords: large multi center study diffusion mri protocols
examination and optimization of ex vivo dwi protocols
topics.title: Multisite Diffusion MRI Protocol Evaluation
topics.description: This cluster centers on evaluating and optimizing diffusion MRI protocols across sites, scanners, and studies. It emphasizes reproducibility, stability, and generalizability of quantitative diffusion measurements, including ex vivo and multi-shell acquisitions.

topics.title: Microstructural Imaging and Cellular Brain Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI and related approaches for probing brain microstructure, tractography, and model-based interpretation of MRI signals. It also links imaging with cellular and molecular mapping methods such as organoids, transcriptomics, and light-sheet microscopy to study cell-type specificity and complex morphology.

topics.title: High-Resolution MRI of Brain Vasculature
topics.description: These studies focus on high-resolution MRI approaches for visualizing, segmenting, and quantifying brain vessels and vascular morphology. They also include advanced MRI techniques for blood velocity and temperature mapping in human brain applications.

keywords: the neural correlates of selective attention
the neural dynamics of simplified mental representations
topics.title: Attention, memory, and reactivation in human cortex
topics.description: This cluster centers on how selective attention and working memory interact with conscious perception, cognitive control, and reactivation of internal representations. It also includes neural correlates from fMRI and related analyses examining prefrontal and network-level dynamics.

keywords: cerebellar functional topography and dynamic interactions underlie episodic memory in age
topics.title: Memory Circuit Changes in Aging and Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on episodic and associative memory, with emphasis on hippocampal, perirhinal, subicular, and cerebellar contributions. It also highlights memory decline and neural signatures associated with aging, mild cognitive impairment, and Alzheimer-related pathology.

topics.title: Hippocampal mechanisms in memory and learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal contributions to episodic, relational, and semantic memory, including consolidation, retrieval, and representation. It also includes navigation and updating processes that shape memory organization across hippocampal subregions and related cortical networks.

topics.title: Robust Brain MRI Segmentation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep-learning and atlas-based approaches for robust brain MRI segmentation, including lesions, glioma, and post-surgical resection cavities. It also includes techniques for improving generalization, quality control, and performance under domain shift.

keywords: spectral wise self supervise deep learning reconstruction
microstructure preserving diffusion mri super resolution
topics.title: Deep Learning and Diffusion MRI Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and diffusion-based methods for accelerated MRI reconstruction, denoising, harmonization, and super-resolution. It also includes related multimodal synthesis and registration tasks across MRI and PET.

keywords: multimodal multi field strength mri dataset
topics.title: Multifield MRI and Synthetic Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on translational neuroimaging across low-, mid-, and ultra-high-field MRI, with emphasis on contrast generation, reconstruction, and image standardization. It also includes quality assurance and dataset-driven approaches supporting clinical neuroscience research.

topics.title: Brain Structure-Function Dynamics and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain network organization, connectivity, and structure-function coupling relate to dynamic brain states and transitions. It spans connectome-level measures, thalamic and cortical connectivity, and variability in functional dynamics across resolutions and conditions.

topics.title: Generalizable EEG Fingerprinting Across Sites
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying robust individual differences and neural fingerprints from multi-site EEG and related data. It emphasizes harmonisation, cross-site variability, and zero-shot generalization for more reliable neurodevelopmental assessment.

topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI and EEG Denoising Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on denoising and quality-control methods for multi-echo fMRI and EEG, including ICA-based, data-driven, and evaluation frameworks. It also includes artifact removal under controlled contamination and signal-quality assessment for EEG and MRI.

keywords: adni resting state functional mri processing quality control
hcp rs fmri pre processing pipeline
group fmri revisited computationally tractable bayesian hierarchical model
topics.title: fMRI Reproducibility and Processing Evaluation
topics.description: This cluster centers on reproducibility, preprocessing, and quality control in fMRI analysis, with emphasis on denoising, bias correction, and pipeline evaluation. It also includes work linking imaging measures to behavior and comparing methods for robust functional connectivity estimation.

topics.title: Dynamic Connectome Modeling for Brain Behavior
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling functional and structural connectomes to predict brain-behavior relationships, often using graph-based and deep learning approaches. It emphasizes dynamic connectivity, representation learning, and fusion models for individualized prediction and mapping.

keywords: task guide cross subject neural alignment
topics.title: Individualized Brain Dynamics and Alignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling individualized brain dynamics from fMRI and dynamic functional connectivity, often using alignment and representation-learning approaches across subjects. It also includes predictive and inferential methods such as hidden Markov models, diffusion models, and statistical testing.

topics.title: Functional Connectivity, Brain States, and Individual Differences
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional and effective connectivity vary across resting and task fMRI, including naturalistic paradigms such as movie watching. It also highlights individual brain organization, stable components, and predictive modelling of memory and other cognitive states.
